session: c8g.metal-24xl, Graviton4 (Neoverse V2), Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507 Q4_K_M, 32k context (~31.8k-token fill prompt), flash attention on, seed 42, -t 96
method: per-process perf stat core PMU; prefill = -n 1, decode = -n 128
counters: IPC, frontend-stall cycles (r23), backend-stall cycles (r24), L1D refills, L2D refills
caveats: System-wide CMN-700 DRAM-bandwidth counter (arm_cmn/hns_mc_reqs_local_all) was verified available on metal but the per-workload capture was unreliable (multiplexed ~66 percent, physically impossible aggregate byte counts); no DRAM GB/s number is cited, the memory-bound conclusion rests on the per-process backend-stall evidence.
L2D refills are higher for q4 than f16; do not lean on the refill-count delta, the backend-stall-cycle reduction is the load-bearing signal.
Metal run used 96 threads for feasibility; absolute throughput numbers in the study are the 16-thread c8g measurements, the mechanism is a thread-count-independent ratio.
SPE memory-access profiling failed (root volume filled mid-record); the core-PMU backend-stall evidence does not need it.
